untranslatable_reason: Twin Peak/Double Top pattern requires detection of two distinct peaks at equal price levels with a valley between them. This structural pattern recognition cannot be expressed using only price-level indicators and mathematical operators. Requires candlestick pattern recognition or peak/valley detection logic not available in the allowed indicators list. The confirmation point is described qualitatively as 'typically at the confirmation line level' without precise definition. Peak-finding algorithms or pattern state machines are needed.
translation_notes: Attempted to approximate the core logic: entry when price closes below confirmation level (modeled as pivot point), exit when price closes above confirmation level. However, this loses the essential pattern structure requirement of identifying two equal peaks with intervening valley. Success rate cited as 72% for bear markets; failure rate of 65% represents cases where price fails to close below confirmation point and reverses higher. The signal inherently requires pattern state tracking across multiple bars, which cannot be reduced to point-in-time indicator comparisons.
